bull.b: Wingstop’s franchised model with high AUVs and sub‑2 year payback creates durable, low‑capital growth. Strong unit economics support ongoing franchise demand, predictable royalty streams and scalable margin expansion as the system grows, reinforcing a capital‑light expansion engine.

Sustained high operating and EBITDA margins paired with solid operating cash flow and meaningful free cash flow provide long‑term flexibility. Reliable cash generation funds dividends, buybacks and reinvestment while insulating operations from cyclical pressures over months to quarters.

bull.t: Accelerating Unit Growth And Large Pipeline
bull.b: Rapid net unit additions and a multi‑thousand unit pipeline underpin recurring royalty growth and scale advantages. Continued global expansion (including planned India entry) should drive durable system‑wide sales growth and stronger network effects over the medium term.

bear.b: Persistently negative equity constrains financial flexibility, elevates solvency perception risk and can limit strategic options. Even with recent debt reduction, negative equity increases vulnerability to shocks and may complicate capital allocation over the medium term.

Material comp weakness reduces royalty flow and stresses franchise economics when prolonged. If consumer pressure persists, franchisee profitability and AUV improvement assumptions may be delayed, slowing royalty growth and pressuring system cash generation over coming quarters.

bear.t: Execution Risk: Smart Kitchen Rollout Incomplete
bear.b: Operational initiatives like Smart Kitchen improved metrics but remain far from systemwide adoption. Incomplete rollout creates inconsistent service and delivery economics across restaurants, risking customer experience and slower realization of labor/throughput benefits that underpin margin gains.
